Co Pilot Collections ManagerPrivate CRM for collection teams

Work accounts, track promises, collect payments, and monitor your team from one clean system.

Built for small collection teams that need a simple collector workflow: import accounts, assign employees, work one debtor at a time, schedule callbacks, manage payment plans, generate branded letters, and track activity.

📥 ImportUpload CSV portfolios and map headers.
☎ Work QueuePhone-first search, notes, calls, and statuses.
💳 PromisesPayment plans, due dashboard, broken promises.
📄 LettersProfessional debtor PDFs with logo watermark.
👥 StaffApprove employees, assign accounts, monitor work.
🧾 AuditTrack activity, payments, notes, and changes.

Smart Account Assignment

Admin only. Choose employees, choose how many accounts to assign, then filter/sort by state, city, balance, portfolio, status, or random distribution.
Admin can select one or multiple signed-up employees. Hold Command on Mac or Ctrl on Windows to select multiple.
Employees must appear here from real signup + admin approval. Manual employee entry is disabled.
No approved employees yet. Employees must sign up first, then admin must approve them in Manage Employees.

How to Use Co Pilot Collections Manager

Simple daily workflow Keep collectors focused: search, call, status, note, promise, follow-up, next account.
1. Import accounts

Admin imports a CSV, maps columns, checks the account list, then assigns accounts to employees.

2. Work the queue

Collectors open their queue, search primarily by phone number, click a debtor, call, status, and notate.

3. Use Today

The Today Dashboard shows callbacks, promises due, overdue payments, and broken promises.

4. Payment plans

Create payment plans tied to the current balance. Record payments so balances and plan totals update.

5. Generate letters

Create debtor letters/PDFs with the company logo watermark. Use sample/test data when training staff.

6. Manage staff

Admin approves employees, assigns accounts, monitors activity, and removes fired employees.

Before showing a prospect

App Branding Settings

Admin can customize the app name, the small subtitle under the name, and the logo shown in the top-left navigation. This is separate from the letter watermark/logo.

Promise Automation + Collector Alerts

Collector command alerts
Auto-detect broken promises, promises due today, upcoming promises, payment activity, high-balance follow-ups, and accounts that need fast action.
Automation checks payment promises and payment plan schedules. Run the SQL file once so alerts can also save to Supabase.

Payment Ledger / Record Payment

This is the money ledger. Completed payments reduce the current account balance. Reversals add the amount back. Each entry creates a permanent payment record and audit entry.

Edit Letter Body Templates

Admin can edit the body paragraphs for each letter type. Use placeholders like {{debtorName}}, {{currentBalance}}, {{dueDate}}, {{accountNumber}}, {{originalCreditor}}, and {{authorizedBy}}. Separate paragraphs with a blank line.
System default
Available placeholders: {{debtorName}} {{accountNumber}} {{fileNumber}} {{originalCreditor}} {{currentBalance}} {{settlementAmount}} {{dueDate}} {{docDate}} {{initialPayment}} {{installmentAmount}} {{frequency}} {{paymentMethod}} {{cardMask}} {{lastPaymentAmount}} {{lastPaymentDate}} {{minimumDue}} {{returnedAmount}} {{authorizedBy}}

Debtor Document Generator

Employee chooses the document type, reviews the auto-filled debtor/account values, then downloads the PDF and/or saves the document record to the account. No company name, company address, or company email is printed on the letters.
Company Logo for Letters
Admin upload only. Logo is shared with employees and appears as a professional transparent watermark on downloaded debtor letters.
No logo uploaded yet.
Best file: PNG or JPG logo. The app keeps the logo proportional and places it as a faint centered watermark behind the letter text.

This is internal communication only. Do not send full card numbers, CVV, full routing/account numbers, or sensitive payment data.

Payment Promises & Admin Confirmation

Manual payment tracking — no card/ACH processing inside the app.
Employees record payment promises using amount, date, method, and last 4 only. Admin processes in the outside merchant portal, then marks Paid, Failed, NSF, Canceled, or Rescheduled.

Compliance Guard / Call Rules

Open an account to review compliance rules.
Call protection rules
• Blocks DNC, cease & desist, bankruptcy, deceased, attorney represented, disputed/frozen, and wrong-number accounts.
• Warns or blocks outside the account state/time-zone call window.
• Warns or blocks after the daily call limit is reached.
• Admin can override a block with a saved compliance override note.

Pricing & Monthly Licensing

Choose a private agency installation with its own database, branded subdomain, users, portfolios, reports, documents, Client Portal, and remittance accounting.

Download Pricing PDF
Simple plans for small and growing collection agenciesEvery paying agency receives a separate private installation. The Agency plan is the recommended starting point for most teams.
Pricing effective July 2026
For small agencies

Starter

$299/month
$750 setup fee
  • Up to 25,000 accounts
  • 3 active staff users
  • 1 administrator
  • 2 client/portfolio owner logins
  • Private database and agency subdomain
  • Company branding and logo
  • Imports, payment plans and settlements
  • Reports, documents and receipts
  • Team Messenger and Client Portal
  • Remittance accounting
  • Standard email support
MOST POPULAR

Agency

$599/month
$1,500 setup fee
  • Up to 100,000 accounts
  • 10 active staff users
  • Up to 10 client logins
  • Everything in Starter
  • Priority onboarding
  • One initial portfolio import
  • Staff configuration
  • Branding setup
  • Remittance and fee schedule setup
  • Two training sessions
  • Standard custom document setup
For growing agencies

Agency Plus

$999/month
$2,500 setup fee
  • Up to 250,000 accounts
  • 20 active staff users
  • Up to 25 client logins
  • Everything in Agency
  • Multiple portfolio imports
  • Advanced onboarding
  • Priority support
  • Custom reports and document templates
  • Quarterly system review
  • Assisted data exports
  • Higher storage allowance
Custom scale

Enterprise

From $1,500/month
$5,000+ setup fee
  • More than 250,000 accounts
  • More than 20 staff users
  • Multiple operating teams
  • Custom storage and compute
  • Custom reporting
  • Integration work
  • Enhanced backup options
  • Dedicated onboarding
  • Negotiated support agreement

Founding Agency Program

Limited introductory pricing may be available to the first five agencies that provide structured product feedback during their first year. Contact sales for eligibility and written terms.

Private by designEach agency receives its own Supabase project, deployment, users, data, and subdomain.
No shared employee loginsNamed users preserve accountability, audit history, and collector reporting.
Guided onboardingSetup fees cover provisioning, configuration, migration, testing, branding, and training.
Pricing is subject to final agreement, onboarding review, data volume, infrastructure usage, integrations, and requested customization. Setup fees are non-refundable once provisioning begins.
